experiments.description: Baseline SAC+HER, unmodified. 256x3 MLP, batch 256, buffer 200k, HER k=4 future, STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000, UPDATE_EVERY=1, N_UPDATES=1.
experiments.hypothesis: Baseline establishes the reference point. Expect low success_rate because the 10s budget combined with STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000 leaves very few gradient updates available for SAC to actually learn anything.
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Robot ignores the goal entirely; arm collapses downward into the table across all 3 episodes. In ep1 the gripper actually starts within the success threshold of the goal but the deterministic policy actively walks AWAY from it. Policy is effectively untrained.
vlm_failure_modes: walks away from goal (no goal-tracking behavior)
arm collapses into table (strong downward bias in policy output)
ignores desired_goal portion of obs (similar end pose across different goals)
fails to exploit favorable starts (ep1 starts at goal, policy still leaves)
vlm_suggestions: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 1000 -> 100 (single highest-leverage change, doubles available gradient updates)
N_UPDATES 1 -> 4 (REDQ-style UTD, multiplies effective gradient signal)
dense reward shaping in training (-distance instead of sparse -1/0) for stronger gradient signal
smaller network (128x2 or 64x2) since obs is only 13-dim
experiments.lesson_learned: On a 10s wall-clock budget, the STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000 random warmup consumes ~54% of total env steps before any gradient update fires. Only 853 SAC updates total, far too few to train a 410k-param net even on FetchReach. The dominant constraint here is gradient steps, not env experience.

experiments.description: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 1000 -> 100. Single-variable change to test whether earlier learning lifts gradient-update count within 10s budget.
experiments.hypothesis: Lowering the random pre-learning warmup from 1000 to 100 will roughly double total_updates within the same 10s budget (expecting ~1700 from ~1850 total steps), enough to lift success_rate above 0.0.
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Robot still ignores goals; no goal-tracking behavior visible. ep2 shows a new failure mode (retract arm upward) not seen in exp1, but ep0/ep1 still collapse into the table. mean_distance dropped 0.61 -> 0.40 but driven by random end-pose distribution, not by reaching toward goals.
vlm_failure_modes: new failure: arm retracts upward into vertical posture (ep2)
policy direction is inconsistent across episodes — different default poses for similar starts
vlm_suggestions: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING change is neutral for total_updates; don't pursue alone
next attack: dense reward shaping in replay buffer (-distance instead of sparse)
if dense reward unlocks >0% success, layer UTD>1 (N_UPDATES=2-4) on exp 4
experiments.lesson_learned: GPU throughput per gradient update is the binding constraint, not env-step count. Random pre-learning rollouts are essentially free (no sample, no grad), so reducing them barely affects total_updates (853 -> 890, +4.3%). Total env steps DROP (1853 -> 990) because once learning starts, every step pays for action sampling + grad update, slowing env-step rate to ~90 steps/sec. To get more updates in 10s, need faster grad steps (smaller net/batch) OR better sample efficiency per step (dense reward, n-step returns, REDQ/DroQ).

experiments.description: Dense reward shaping in replay buffer: replace sparse {-1, 0} per-transition reward with -L2 distance(next_achieved, post-HER desired). Eval reward (env-defined) unchanged.
experiments.hypothesis: Dense reward gives nonzero gradient signal on every transition (vs sparse, which is informative only within 5cm of goal). Expected first non-zero success_rate of the run.
changes_made: sample(): override stored env reward with dense reward computed from buffer next_achieved_goals + post-HER desired_goals (consistent regardless of HER fraction)
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Behavior visually indistinguishable from baseline. Same 'arm collapses or retracts to default pose, ignores goal' failure across all 3 episodes. Dense reward changed signal quality but not training outcome.
vlm_failure_modes: arm retracts to LEFT/DOWN default pose regardless of goal location
deterministic eval policy outputs near-fixed action regardless of obs
vlm_suggestions: Root cause: too few grad updates (838) for SAC to converge. Auto-alpha starts at exp(0)=1.0 so entropy term DOMINATES actor loss in early training, regardless of reward signal quality.
Fix: attack update throughput directly. Smaller net (128x2) + smaller batch (128) should give ~3-5x more updates in 10s.
Dense reward is plausibly correct but insufficient alone — keep as a layered change for after smaller-net breaks 0%.
experiments.lesson_learned: Reward signal quality doesn't help when there are too few gradient updates for SAC to converge. With auto_alpha starting at alpha=1.0 and FetchReach Q-values O(-10), the entropy regularization term dominates the Q term in actor_loss for the first several thousand updates. Until alpha anneals down (which itself takes updates), the actor is being pushed toward random actions regardless of how clean the Q-target is. The 838-update budget is fundamentally pre-convergence.

experiments.description: Shrink net 256x3 -> 128x2 (~13x fewer params) and halve batch 256 -> 128 to multiply grad-step throughput.
experiments.hypothesis: Three previous 0% experiments suggest gradient-step count is the binding constraint. Shrinking the actor+critic and the batch should give 3-5x more updates within 10s, expected to break 0% success_rate.
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Different end-pose pattern (ep0 arm now EXTENDS outward instead of collapsing inward) but still no goal-tracking. mean_distance 0.52 (worse than exp2/3, in noise band). VRAM dropped 27->19 MB confirming smaller net, but total_updates only rose from 853 to 928 (+9%), far below predicted 3-5x.
vlm_failure_modes: ep0 shows new failure mode: arm extends past goal (overshooting baseline rest pose)
ep1, ep2 still poke down at table center
vlm_suggestions: Architectural shrinking is dead end on this setup — Python overhead dominates per-update cost, not GPU FLOPs.
Total updates appears capped at ~900-950 in 10s regardless of net size.
Pivot strategy: make each update count more. Attack the actor signal-to-noise problem with low fixed alpha + dense reward.
AUTO_ALPHA=False + INIT_ALPHA=0.05 + dense reward shaping is the next attack.
experiments.lesson_learned: GPU FLOPs are NOT the dominant cost per gradient step on this setup. Shrinking the net 13x and the batch 2x only yielded +9% updates per second. The bottleneck is Python overhead in the per-step loop: HER's per-sample Python for-loop in buffer.sample(), per-step action sampling with .cpu().numpy() round-trips, buffer.add() episode tracking. Total grad updates per 10s is roughly capped at ~900-950 regardless of architecture choices. To make progress, optimize per-update QUALITY not quantity.

insights: Gradient-step budget on a 10s wall-clock is ~850-900 updates regardless of STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING. The binding constraint is GPU throughput per grad step (~85-90 upd/s), NOT env-step count. Random pre-learning steps are free; learning steps cost ~10ms each.
Total_steps is misleading on this setup — exp1 had 1853 total steps but only 853 grad updates because the first 1000 were free random rollouts. Don't optimize total_steps; optimize total_updates.

Reward signal quality (sparse vs dense) doesn't move the needle when the gradient-step budget is too small. Auto-alpha starts at exp(0)=1.0 and the entropy term dominates the Q-term in actor_loss for the first ~1000-2000 updates regardless of reward shape. Need to attack #updates first (smaller net) or bypass entropy domination (lower/fixed alpha).
failed_directions: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 1000 -> 100 alone (exp2): only +4% updates, 0% success_rate. The lever was wrong; not the warmup but the per-update cost.

Dense reward shaping alone (exp3): 0% success_rate; behavior visually unchanged. Reward signal quality cannot compensate for too-few gradient updates. Auto-alpha at init=1.0 means entropy term dominates actor loss until alpha anneals — and that itself takes more updates than 838.
Smaller net (128x2) + smaller batch (128) (exp4): only +9% updates, 0% success_rate. GPU FLOPs are not the bottleneck; Python overhead in per-step loop is. Architectural shrinking is a dead end here.
promising_directions: BUNDLE: low fixed alpha (AUTO_ALPHA=False, INIT_ALPHA=0.05) + dense reward — attacks actor signal-to-noise directly. Synergistic.
If bundle works: ablate to find minimum (low alpha alone? dense reward alone with low alpha?)
If bundle fails: pivot to TD3 (deterministic, no entropy term, better fit for FetchReach + HER)
Behavioral cloning bootstrap: warmup actor with supervised regression to (goal - achieved) direction
